entities.name: Credential
entities.role: A license, certification, background check, training record, or right-to-work document that a worker must hold to be eligible for a shift.

transitions.action: verification fails or document never received
waiver lifted; new credential on file

capabilities.name: View per-worker credential dashboard
capabilities.note: Workforce list shows Credential status filter button and a 'Credential watch' count. Per-worker detail renders a full credential accordion with status per item (ok/warn/err/missing/review), expiry dates, and source attribution.

capabilities.name: List and filter workers by credential status
capabilities.note: Workforce list has a 'Credential status' filter chip that filters by Compliant / Onboarding / Expired. Credential watch summary count visible on workforce header.

capabilities.name: View credentialing compliance dashboard (program-level)
capabilities.note: Dashboard shows 'Credential watch' summary widget and an OSHA-10 expiry alert with 'Suspend 12 future shifts' action. Help center confirms a dedicated Credentialing/Compliance section in the side nav with per-industry credential packs and expiry KPI tiles.

capabilities.name: Request credential renewal from supplier/worker
capabilities.note: Per-credential row shows 'Request now' (err/missing) or 'Renew' (warn) action button. Bulk 'Request credentials' action on workforce list. 'Send renewal reminder' button present in credential detail actions.

capabilities.name: Export credential file for a worker
capabilities.note: 'Export credential file' button present in per-worker credentialing detail action bar.

capabilities.name: Runtime booking block on expired/missing credential
capabilities.note: Dashboard shows 'OSHA-10 expired for Terry Donin. Suspend 12 future shifts' alert. Source confirms credentialSummary() drives a 'fail' level that blocks booking. Help center states 'hard-block on assignments past expiry' and 'blocks ineligible bookings'.

capabilities.name: Auto-suspend shift assignments when credential expires
capabilities.note: Source and dashboard both confirm auto-suspension of bookings/shifts when a credential reaches expired state. Multiple industry packs show 'Expired X — bookings suspended / auto-suspended from power-equipment shifts'.

capabilities.name: Primary-source verification (trigger third-party check)
capabilities.note: Help center describes PSV (NPDB / state board pulls) as a now-in-flight roadmap item, but the prototype already renders PSV status rows (e.g., 'PSV — CA Board verified Apr 22 2026') and a 'Verify OSHA cards' / 'Cross-checking against State ABC Board' action in the credentialing dashboard. Rendered as present because the render shows it.

capabilities.name: Create / add a new credential record for a worker
capabilities.note: VMS systems must allow operators or suppliers to manually add a new credential (e.g., upload a certification PDF, record a license number and expiry). No create/upload affordance is visible in any walk screen or source for adding a net-new credential entry to a worker's file; only renewal requests and status reads are evidenced.

capabilities.name: Upload supporting document for a credential
capabilities.note: Standard VMS credentialing requires attaching the source document (PDF certificate, ID scan) to the credential record for audit purposes. No file-upload control is visible in the prototype walk or source. Help center references 'supplier-attested PDFs' as an existing mechanism but no UI for uploading them is rendered.

capabilities.name: Edit credential details (expiry date, license number, notes)
capabilities.note: Credential records display expiry dates and license numbers but no edit control is visible for correcting or updating a credential's metadata (common when a renewal is processed manually or a data-entry error must be fixed).

capabilities.name: Delete or remove a credential from a worker's file
capabilities.note: VMS programs need the ability to remove a credential that was added in error or that no longer applies (e.g., role change). No delete affordance is evidenced in any screen or source file.

capabilities.name: Archive / retire a credential type from the catalog
capabilities.note: Compliance programs evolve; credential types become obsolete (regulatory change, role elimination). Standard VMS practice is to retire rather than delete a catalog entry to preserve historical records. No archive control is evidenced.

capabilities.name: Grant waiver / compliance exception for a credential
capabilities.note: VMS programs serving large programs always need a waiver/exception path (e.g., a worker in a grace period, or a credential accepted by one site but not another). No waiver or exception-grant UI is visible; enforcement mode toggles exist at the policy level but not per-worker credential instance.

capabilities.name: Configure credential policy enforcement (block vs. warn) per credential type
capabilities.note: Source contains a 'blockWindow' toggle ('Block bookings when expired') and enforcement policy objects with 'block' and 'warn' levels, and history entries showing admin tightening auto-suspend thresholds. This is in the settings/policy layer rather than a visible prototype screen, so classified partial.

capabilities.name: Configure per-site training matrix (site-specific credential requirements)
capabilities.note: Help center describes site-scoped credential status and a 'Per-site training matrix' as a NEXT roadmap item. Source references site-scoped credential variants. No builder UI for configuring per-site requirements is rendered in the walk; classified partial because the concept is surfaced but the management UI is roadmap.

capabilities.name: Supervisor override gate at clock-in for expired credential
capabilities.note: Help center roadmap ('NOW') explicitly states 'Expired credential at clock-in time blocks the punch with a supervisor override gate'. The feature is in-flight but no UI for the override itself is rendered in the prototype walk.

capabilities.name: Credential auto-renewal task routing (day-30/15/3 escalation)
capabilities.note: Help center roadmap ('NEXT') calls out structured renewal task routing with day-30/15/3 escalation to worker and supplier. Currently only manual renewal requests are evidenced; automated task routing is not yet rendered.

entities.name: Invoice
entities.role: A supplier billing document generated from approved timesheets, line-itemised by worker and site, that finance tracks through to payment.

capabilities.name: Auto-generate invoice from approved timesheets
capabilities.note: Source confirms invoices are system-generated from approved TimePairs; help center describes auto-generation as the standard frontline-program variation.

capabilities.name: View invoice list with status filtering
Search invoices by invoice ID, supplier, or site
capabilities.note: Search field explicitly labeled 'Search for invoice, supplier, site'.

capabilities.name: View invoice detail (line items, amounts, supplier billing info)
Mark invoice paid
capabilities.note: 'Mark paid' button present on both invoice detail and as a bulk action on the list.

capabilities.name: Send invoice to AP
capabilities.note: 'Send to AP' bulk action in source transitions Generated invoices toward Issued/AP processing.

capabilities.name: Raise dispute on invoice
capabilities.note: Dispute action present as both a bulk action and a per-invoice context-menu item (source line 1124). No separate Disputed state is rendered in the UI — the action fires a toast only; the full dispute workflow (supplier response, resolution, credit note) is not evidenced.

capabilities.name: Void invoice
capabilities.note: Void appears as a bulk action in source (line 414). No Voided status tab or detail-screen affordance is rendered; effect after voiding is not shown.

capabilities.name: Download invoice PDF
capabilities.note: Download PDF button present on invoice detail and as per-row and bulk actions on the list.

capabilities.name: Export invoice list (CSV / PDF)
capabilities.note: 'Export to CSV' and 'Export to PDF' toolbar actions present in source.

capabilities.name: Print invoice / remittance
capabilities.note: Print action present on invoice detail toolbar and as a bulk overflow action ('Print remittance').

capabilities.name: Defer invoice to next billing cycle
capabilities.note: 'Defer to next cycle' present as a bulk overflow action in source (line 419). No detail-screen affordance or resulting state change is rendered.

capabilities.name: Attach note to AP on invoice
capabilities.note: 'Attach note to AP' present as a bulk overflow action (line 418) and 'Add payment note' on detail (line 1120). Both fire a toast; no persistent notes panel is rendered.

capabilities.name: Issue credit note for corrected or retroactive invoice
capabilities.note: VMS AP workflows require credit notes when approved invoices need retroactive correction (e.g. timesheet edit after payment). Roadmap explicitly lists 'Auto-credit-note for retroactive timesheet edits' as a NEXT item, confirming it is expected but not yet present in the prototype.

capabilities.name: ERP / accounting system posting
capabilities.note: The help center notes invoices should post to the ERP; source comment (line 388-389) describes 'ship to ERP' as an AP job-to-be-done. Roadmap lists 'Direct ERP webhook posting' as NEXT. No UI affordance for ERP export or posting confirmation is rendered.

capabilities.name: Consolidated buyer invoice (multi-supplier roll-up)
capabilities.note: Help center documents consolidated buyer invoicing as a standard VMS variation for MSP programs (a single rolled-up bill across all suppliers). No such aggregation or consolidated-invoice generation screen is present in the prototype.

capabilities.name: Supplier-submitted invoice upload / reconciliation
capabilities.note: Help center documents supplier-submitted invoices as a standard variation for professional/SOW programs. No upload, intake, or exception-reconciliation workflow for supplier-submitted invoices is evidenced in the prototype.

capabilities.name: Filter/sort invoice list by supplier or site
capabilities.note: Supplier and Sites filter buttons visible on the invoice list toolbar.

entities.name: Rate Card
entities.role: The configuration object that governs bill rates, pay rates, markups, and premium rules; resolved at booking time for every requisition and timesheet.

transitions.action: publish / set effective date in the past
superseded by a newer active version

capabilities.name: Create rate card version
capabilities.note: RcCreateVersionPanel and createRateCardVersion() are confirmed in source; UI button 'Create new version' is visible in RateCardsManager.

capabilities.name: View rate card detail (rows, version info, status)
capabilities.note: RateCardsManager renders version bar with label, effective date, status pill, job count, and a searchable base-pay table per version.

capabilities.name: List and browse all versions
capabilities.note: RateCardsManager versions table shows all versions with status chips; a dropdown selector also provides version navigation.

capabilities.name: Search / filter job rows within a rate card
capabilities.note: A 'Search jobs' text input inside the base-pay table filters rows by job name in real time.

capabilities.name: Edit rate card rows (base pay, season uplift)
capabilities.note: setRateCardRow() and RcPayInput / RcUpliftControl inline editors confirmed in source; read-only mode enforced on expired versions.

capabilities.name: Update version metadata (label, effective date, note)
capabilities.note: updateVersionMeta() and RcCreateVersionPanel (label + effectiveFrom fields) confirmed in source.

capabilities.name: Delete a non-active rate card version
capabilities.note: deleteRateCardVersion() with a confirm dialog ('Delete version') confirmed in source and RateCardsManager; active version cannot be deleted.

capabilities.name: Archive a rate card
capabilities.note: VMS rate cards accumulate over multi-year programs; archiving retired cards while preserving them for audit/retrospective billing is a standard compliance need. No archive action or archived state found in source or walk.

capabilities.name: Activate / promote a version (make it the current active version)
capabilities.note: Activation is date-driven: a version whose effectiveFrom <= today is automatically treated as active by rcVersionStatus(). No explicit 'Activate' button is present; a user promotes a version by setting its effectiveFrom to today or the past via the Create/edit flow. The intent is evidenced but the explicit user action is implicit.

capabilities.name: Schedule a future rate card rollout
capabilities.note: A version with effectiveFrom in the future gets status='scheduled' and is recognized in the UI. However, the roadmap item 'Rate-card versioning + scheduled rollouts' (prio: later) mentions a diff view and approval gate not yet present, indicating the scheduling mechanism is basic (date-only) rather than fully supported.

capabilities.name: Set location / org-node overrides on rate card rows
capabilities.note: setNodeOverride(), clearNodeOverrides(), and RcNodeRowEditor confirmed in source; NodeRateCardBody renders per-node overrides in the org tree.

capabilities.name: Set supplier-specific (agency) rate overrides
capabilities.note: setAgencyOverride(), resolveAgencyRateCard(), and the RateCardsCard component with supplier rate override UI confirmed in source.

capabilities.name: Compare rate cards across suppliers
capabilities.note: SupCompareRatesPanel with 'Compare rate cards' title confirmed in source; triggered from the supplier list via 'Compare rates' action.

capabilities.name: Export rate card to CSV
capabilities.note: RcExportPanel confirmed in source with CSV download; Export button present in RateCardsManager.

capabilities.name: Import rate card from CSV
capabilities.note: RcImportPanel with CSV mapping confirmed in source; Import button present in RateCardsManager (disabled on expired versions).

capabilities.name: Rate card simulator (preview spend impact before saving)
capabilities.note: The help center explicitly lists 'Rate-card simulator' as a NOW roadmap item ('Preview a rate-card change against the last 30 days of activity — see total spend delta, supplier-by-supplier delta, and worker take-home delta before saving'). No simulator UI was found in the walk or source.

capabilities.name: Approval gate for rate card changes
capabilities.note: Standard VMS governance requires finance or procurement sign-off before a rate change takes effect system-wide; the roadmap item 'Rate-card versioning + scheduled rollouts' mentions an approval gate as future work. No approval workflow for rate card publishing was found in source or walk.

capabilities.name: Automatic minimum-wage compliance tracking
capabilities.note: The help center lists 'Automatic minimum-wage tracking' as a NOW roadmap item. No wage-floor subscription or auto-update UI was found in the walk; checkPayFloor() exists in source as a per-row check only, not a full compliance tracking feature.

capabilities.name: Audit history / change log for rate card edits
capabilities.note: RateCardsCard includes a history panel (historyOpen state, history tabs 'flat' and 'diff' modes) and seeded changelog entries confirmed in source (e.g. 'Initial load from rate_cards_v5.xlsx'). However, the walk-rendered help page does not surface a dedicated history UI; evidenced only in source code.

entities.name: Supplier
entities.role: A staffing agency or vendor that provides workers to fill requisitions, scored by fill rate and spend, and governed by tier and distribution rules.

capabilities.name: Invite supplier
capabilities.note: Invite button on SuppliersPage; two suppliers (Shifty, QuickStaff) shown in Invited status.

capabilities.name: View supplier list
capabilities.note: Supplier list with search, status filter, spend/worker/location columns visible across multiple screens.

capabilities.name: View supplier detail
capabilities.note: SupplierDetailsPage component present with profile hero, contact info, and activity log.

capabilities.name: Edit supplier profile
capabilities.note: Edit supplier row action is present in the UI; no form or field-level edit screen is rendered in the walk.

capabilities.name: Terminate supplier
capabilities.note: Terminate supplier action with confirmation dialog present; Hireling shown in Terminated status.

capabilities.name: Activate / reinstate supplier
capabilities.note: Activate supplier action shown on Terminated rows as an alternative to Terminate.

capabilities.name: Change supplier tier
capabilities.note: Re-tier bulk action present; tier badges (Tier 1/2/3/Probation/Pending) rendered across list and scorecard.

capabilities.name: View supplier scorecard
capabilities.note: Analytics page shows a full scorecard table (spend, fill rate, workers). SupplierScorecard component exists on detail page but is commented out ('removed for now'). Score data is fully modeled in source.

capabilities.name: Configure supplier distribution rules
capabilities.note: Distribute bulk action present on supplier list. Help center documents tier-and-hold logic thoroughly. No distribution rule editor screen is rendered in the walk.

capabilities.name: Compare supplier rate cards
capabilities.note: Compare rate cards panel implemented (SupCompareRatesPanel) showing side-by-side bill rates across up to three active suppliers.

capabilities.name: Manage supplier contract / rate card
capabilities.note: getSupplierContract accessor and contract renewal calendar implemented in source. No dedicated contract edit screen is walked; onEditContract callback is wired but destination not shown.

capabilities.name: Message / contact supplier
capabilities.note: messageSupplier action available via the AI assistant; no dedicated supplier messaging UI screen is rendered in the walk.

capabilities.name: Archive supplier
capabilities.note: VMS programs commonly soft-archive terminated suppliers to retain spend history and audit trail while removing them from active distribution queues. The prototype uses Terminated status but shows no archive/hide affordance distinct from termination.

capabilities.name: Onboard supplier (complete MSA / compliance intake)
capabilities.note: Onboarding is referenced in the activity log text ('Onboarding in progress') and OFAC / sanctions screening is modeled in source fields, but no onboarding wizard or checklist screen is rendered in the walk. Expected in VMS to capture MSA, insurance certs, diversity classification, and payment terms before a supplier goes Active.

capabilities.name: Suspend supplier (temporary hold)
capabilities.note: VMS programs routinely suspend a supplier temporarily (e.g., during a compliance investigation) without terminating the relationship. The prototype offers only Terminate; no suspend/hold transition is evidenced.

capabilities.name: Export supplier performance report
capabilities.note: QBR pack / performance export is explicitly listed on the product roadmap (052_internal-help-center.txt) as a Next item ('Supplier QBR pack auto-generation'). No export action is present in the current prototype.

entities.name: Shift
entities.role: A single time-bounded work slot within a requisition that can be open (unfilled) or assigned to a named worker.

transitions.action: broadcast to suppliers (tiered or open market)
transitions.note: Broadcast pipeline described in help center (049) and source (110_d6271132.js); no explicit UI control rendered in walk screens — scheduling console shows result (open/assigned) but not the broadcast action itself.
Worker mobile (097_43455436.js) shows Accept button on shift detail; toast 'Shift accepted' fires on confirm.
Worker mobile (097_43455436.js) shows Decline and Opt-out buttons on shift detail.
Worker schedule in 092_2a61a622.js shows Confirmed status for tomorrow's shifts; source confirms 'confirmed' field on shift rows.

transitions.action: worker taps I'm on my way
transitions.note: Worker mobile (097_43455436.js) has onIAmOnMyWayOpen handler; triggers travel sheet.
Source (055_dad1a3d6.js) defines floor status with clock-in logic; menu shows 'End shift now' only for floor/wrapping/break rows.
Source (055_dad1a3d6.js) shows 'Start break' menu item for floor rows and 'End break' for break rows.
Source (055_dad1a3d6.js): 'End break — back on the floor' toast.
Source (055_dad1a3d6.js) defines wrapping status in the status order enum.
Source (055_dad1a3d6.js) 'End shift now' action; closed status maps to label 'Completed'.
Source (055_dad1a3d6.js) shows 'Mark no-show' menu item and bulk action; toast '...marked no-show — supplier and site notified'.
VMS commonly allows a scheduler to cancel an open shift that is no longer needed before it is filled; no explicit cancel-shift UI found for open positions in scheduler console.
Source (055_dad1a3d6.js): 'Remove from shift' action reopens slot to supplier tier; body text confirms.
Dashboard activity (000) shows 'OSHA-10 expired for Terry Donin — Suspend 12 future shifts'; source (061, 102) confirms auto-suspend on expired credential.
Source (055_dad1a3d6.js): 'File incident report' action sends flag to supplier/admin; shift remains in place.
Dashboard (000) shows 'Shift auto-closed — 1 Production Associate'; source (055_dad1a3d6.js) derives closed status from day.isPast for open shifts.

capabilities.name: Create shift
capabilities.note: Dashboard shortcut 'Create new shift' is present in 000; permissions source (073) defines 'sch.create — Place new bookings and shifts'; however the actual creation form/modal is not walked, so the full creation flow is unconfirmed.

capabilities.name: View shift list / schedule console
capabilities.note: Calendar and List views of the schedule are walked across multiple days; By worker / Sites toggle and Day view are evidenced.

capabilities.name: Filter and group shifts
capabilities.note: Source (055_dad1a3d6.js) implements locFilter (site-id array) and groupBy (worker / booking / location / supplier / status) on the list view.

capabilities.name: View shift detail
capabilities.note: Shift cards are clickable in both calendar and list views (onOpenShift handler in source 055); a detail screen exists for assigned shifts (source 097 worker app) but the scheduler-side shift detail page is not directly walked.

capabilities.name: Edit shift (time, role, location)
capabilities.note: Source (055_dad1a3d6.js) includes 'Edit shift...' menu item that fires a toast; no edit form is rendered in the walk, so the fields editable are not confirmed.

capabilities.name: Delete shift
capabilities.note: VMS commonly allows permanent deletion of draft or future shifts that were created in error; no delete affordance found in scheduler menus or source — only 'remove worker' (unassign) and auto-close are evidenced.

capabilities.name: Broadcast shift to suppliers (tiered / open market)
capabilities.note: Help center (049) describes the broadcast pipeline (offered → accepted) and five broadcast variations; source (110_d6271132.js) confirms tiered-hold logic; however the broadcast trigger UI is not rendered in the walk screens.

capabilities.name: Assign worker to open shift
capabilities.note: Source (055_dad1a3d6.js): 'Assign worker...' menu item on open-position rows opens a worker picker.

capabilities.name: Unassign / remove worker from shift
capabilities.note: Source (055_dad1a3d6.js): 'Remove from shift' confirms action — slot reopens to supplier tier, existing time entries preserved.

capabilities.name: Worker accept shift invite
capabilities.note: Worker mobile (097_43455436.js) Accept button on shift detail; 000 shows upcoming assignments with confirmed % counter.

capabilities.name: Worker decline shift invite
capabilities.note: Source (097_43455436.js): Decline and Opt-out buttons on shift detail with reason-capture sheet.

capabilities.name: Worker signal I'm on my way
capabilities.note: Source (097_43455436.js): onIAmOnMyWayOpen handler triggers travel sheet; moves shift to enroute.

capabilities.name: Clock in / start shift
capabilities.note: Source (055_dad1a3d6.js): floor status is the active-on-site state; menu items branch on floor/break/wrapping rows vs pre-clock-in rows.

capabilities.name: Mark break start / end
capabilities.note: Source (055_dad1a3d6.js): 'Start break' and 'End break — back on the floor' menu items on floor/break rows.

capabilities.name: End shift / clock out
capabilities.note: Source (055_dad1a3d6.js): 'End shift now' confirmation dialog on active rows; status transitions to closed/Completed.

capabilities.name: Mark no-show
capabilities.note: Source (055_dad1a3d6.js): 'Mark no-show' per-row and bulk action; supplier and site notified; reliability score affected.

capabilities.name: Cancel unfilled shift
capabilities.note: VMS commonly needs a scheduler to cancel an open position before it fills when demand changes; no cancel-shift control found for open shifts in the scheduler console — only worker-side cancel (097, for accepted shifts) and auto-close are evidenced.

capabilities.name: Suspend future shifts on credential expiry
capabilities.note: Dashboard activity (000): 'OSHA-10 expired for Terry Donin — Suspend 12 future shifts'; source (061, 102, 113) confirms auto-suspend from expired credential with manual 'Suspend bookings' action.

capabilities.name: File incident report on shift
capabilities.note: Source (055_dad1a3d6.js): 'File incident report' sends flag to supplier and program admin; shift remains in place; does not remove worker.

capabilities.name: Adjust time entry (override clock-in/out)
capabilities.note: Source (055_dad1a3d6.js): 'Add time' / back-dated time entry panel accessible from shift row menu for both active and past shifts.

capabilities.name: Bulk mark no-show
capabilities.note: Source (055_dad1a3d6.js): BulkActionBar with 'Mark no-show' bulk action; eligible rows filtered to pre-clock-in only.

capabilities.name: Copy shift link
capabilities.note: Source (055_dad1a3d6.js): 'Copy shift link' per-row menu item.

capabilities.name: Archive completed / past shifts
capabilities.note: VMS commonly retains past shifts for audit, billing reconciliation, and trend reporting; no explicit archive/retention UI found — past days show a 'Past' label but there is no management control for archiving shift history.

capabilities.name: Worker cancel accepted shift
capabilities.note: Source (097_43455436.js): cancel-shift bottom sheet accessible from '...' menu on shift detail for upcoming/active shifts.

entities.name: Timesheet
entities.role: The time record for a work assignment capturing actual start, end, and break times that routes through approval before triggering invoicing.

transitions.note: Bulk approve action exists in source (bulkActionsTs); single-record approve button on detail page visible as ts-action-split in source, but no rendered confirm screen is in evidence.
Review state is shown in list and source data; approval from Review state is inferred but no dedicated screen was walked.
Bulk 'Send back' and 'Reject' actions confirmed in source (bulkActionsTs); ts.reject permission string present; no rendered reject-detail screen in evidence.
Source permission 'ts.reject' covers reject from any pre-approval state; no walked screen confirms this specific transition.
Inbox item 'Timesheet returned: Fix and resubmit to release payment' implies a resubmit path; no dedicated resubmit screen was walked.
Worker dispute flow is on the roadmap ('Later') per help-center roadmap; standard VMS practice allows workers to contest approved hours. No evidence of this flow in the current prototype.

'Edit time' button present on closed timesheet detail (TS-91239); source confirms ts.edit permission and credit-note auto-generation on retroactive edit mentioned in help center. Full unlock flow not walked.
VMSes commonly support voiding a timesheet for a no-show or cancelled shift to prevent it from progressing to approval; no evidence of a void action in the prototype.

capabilities.name: Create timesheet (clock-in capture)
capabilities.note: Help center confirms timesheets are auto-created from Dayforce time-clock or supplier punch feeds. Clock-in event visible in activity feed.

capabilities.name: View timesheet detail
capabilities.note: Detail page shows worker, supplier, actual vs scheduled times, break timeline, and status.

capabilities.name: Search and filter timesheets
capabilities.note: Search field and status filter tabs (Open, Pending approval, Review, Closed) visible on list page.

capabilities.name: Edit time entries (correct punches)
capabilities.note: 'Edit time' button present on closed timesheet TS-91239; ts.edit permission string confirmed in source.

capabilities.name: Approve timesheet (single record)
capabilities.note: Action button visible on detail page (ts-action-split class in source); bulk approve confirmed; no rendered single-approve confirmation screen walked.

capabilities.name: Bulk approve timesheets
capabilities.note: Checkbox selection and bulk actions toolbar with Approve action confirmed in source (bulkActionsTs). List toolbar with row checkboxes visible in walk.

capabilities.name: Reject / send back timesheet to supplier
capabilities.note: Bulk 'Reject' and 'Send back' actions confirmed in source; ts.reject permission string present; inbox shows 'Timesheet returned' notification.

capabilities.name: Approve timesheet with exception-only auto-approval
capabilities.note: Help center describes exception-only approval as a per-program toggle currently being flipped on by default; no configuration screen walked.

capabilities.name: Flag / escalate timesheet for audit
capabilities.note: Bulk 'Flag' action (icon: Alert) confirmed in source (bulkActionsTs).

capabilities.name: Adjust hours (bulk adjustment panel)
capabilities.note: Bulk 'Adjust hours' action confirmed in source (bulkActionsTs); no adjustment panel screen walked.

capabilities.name: Export timesheets to CSV
capabilities.note: Bulk 'Export' action (icon: FileDownload) confirmed in source (bulkActionsTs).

capabilities.name: Lock timesheet for payroll
capabilities.note: Bulk overflow action 'Lock for payroll' confirmed in source (bulkOverflowTs); not visible in walk render.

capabilities.name: Worker-initiated dispute of approved timesheet
capabilities.note: Standard VMS feature: workers contest incorrect approved hours via a structured dispute flow. Listed on the product roadmap as 'Later'; not present in the current prototype.

capabilities.name: Delete / void timesheet
capabilities.note: VMSes commonly allow voiding erroneous or duplicate timesheets (e.g. created for a no-show). No delete or void action found in the prototype.

capabilities.name: Archive closed timesheets
capabilities.note: VMS audit requirements mandate long-term retention and archival of closed timesheets. No archive action or archived state is evidenced in the prototype.

entities.name: Client Site
entities.role: A physical client location (warehouse, depot, terminal, etc.) where contingent workers are deployed and against which spend and shifts are tracked.

capabilities.name: Create site
capabilities.note: Source confirms a 'Create location' panel (newLocationSchema) reachable from the omnibar Create button in LocationsPage.

capabilities.name: View site list
capabilities.note: 012_clients.txt shows paginated Sites list with name, status, address, shifts, and spend columns.

capabilities.name: Search and filter sites
capabilities.note: Search input visible in walk; source shows filter chips for Status, Region, Type, and Onboarded.

capabilities.name: View site detail
capabilities.note: Source confirms LocationDetailsPage with hero card, quick-action summary, and accordion sections (Details, Schedule, Time capture, Requisitions, Workforce, Supplier distribution, Rate card, Logs).

capabilities.name: Edit site profile
capabilities.note: Source confirms Edit button on detail page opening locationEditSchema panel with name, address, type, region, operating hours, primary contact, and clock method.

capabilities.name: Delete / remove site
capabilities.note: Source shows a 'Remove' danger action in the org-tree row menu that removes the site from the organization tree.

capabilities.name: Archive site
capabilities.note: Audit log entry shows system-generated archive event ('archived — no active requisitions remain') for Terminated sites, but no explicit user-initiated Archive action is exposed; archiving appears automatic rather than a distinct capability.

capabilities.name: Deactivate site (Active to Terminated)
capabilities.note: Deactivate action present in both the detail page more-menu and the bulk-action bar; confirmed via source toast and confirmation dialog.

capabilities.name: Suspend site (temporary hold)
capabilities.note: VMS commonly supports a reversible Suspended state (e.g. temporary closure, safety review) distinct from Terminated. No evidence in walk or source; only Active, Invited, and Terminated statuses appear in the filter options.

capabilities.name: Reactivate suspended site
capabilities.note: Depends on Suspended state, which is absent. VMS programs occasionally need to re-open sites after seasonal closures or incident holds without full re-onboarding.

capabilities.name: Manage supplier distribution rule
capabilities.note: Source confirms LocDistributionBody with 'Set custom rule' and 'Edit custom rule' / 'Clear override' actions, allowing per-site supplier tiering overrides.

capabilities.name: Configure time capture method
capabilities.note: Source confirms LocTimeCaptureBody with a per-site clock method override (Dayforce Clock / QR Code / Manual) and an edit action that toasts success.

capabilities.name: View site spend and shift analytics
capabilities.note: Analytics screen shows spend by site breakdown; site list shows Shifts and Spend columns per row.

capabilities.name: View site requisitions
capabilities.note: Requisition list shows Site column; source confirms LocRequisitionsBody accordion on site detail page.

capabilities.name: View site workforce
capabilities.note: Source confirms LocWorkforceBody accordion and active-workers quick-action card on site detail page.

capabilities.name: Manage site rate card
capabilities.note: Source shows LocAccordionCard for Rate card section and references NodeRateCardBody component inherited down the org tree (Corporate to Region to District to Site). However, no direct edit action for the rate card is confirmed at the site level in the walk render.

capabilities.name: View site audit log
capabilities.note: Source confirms LocLogsBody accordion showing activation, requisition, invoice, and state-change events.

capabilities.name: Invite suppliers to cover a site
capabilities.note: Audit log shows 'invited 4 suppliers to cover [site]' event in the Invited-state timeline, and the org-tree row menu has a 'Set supplier rule' action. However, no standalone invite-supplier-to-site workflow is directly visible in the walk screens.

capabilities.name: Bulk-export sites
capabilities.note: Bulk-action bar in the source includes an Export action and row-level checkboxes are visible in the walk, but the export output format and scope are not confirmed.

capabilities.name: Assign site to org hierarchy (region / district)
capabilities.note: Region field exists in the create/edit schema and org-tree tabs (Regions, Districts, Sites) appear in the nav; audit log references departmental mapping. However, no explicit re-parent action is evidenced in the walk render.

entities.name: Requisition
entities.role: A staffing order that captures the job, scope, rate, location, dates, and approvals for one or more non-employee positions at a client site.

transitions.note: Help-center text describes a multi-step approval workflow before a req goes to suppliers; source 054 (Workflows) shows approval chains gated on submit. The draft state and explicit submit transition are not rendered in the prototype's list or detail views.

Inbox approval queue (source 042) shows approve/reject actions for requisitions with a 24-hour SLA. Approved transitions result in 'Booked' per STATUS_DF_STATE map (source 063), but the intermediate 'Pending approval' state is not shown as a list status pill.
Inbox (source 042) shows a Reject action that sends the item back to the submitter with a note, but 'Draft' is never surfaced as a returned state on the list.
Activity log (source 040) records 'broadcast to N suppliers'; fulfillment helper in source 063 shows Booked = 0% fill, In progress = mid-flight. Status pills for both states are rendered in the list.
Activity log entry: 'closed out all dates'; Completed status pill rendered in list data (source 063).
Cancel action present in both list row context menu and detail page overflow menu with confirmation dialog. Toast confirms cancellation.
Cancel requisition action available on detail page for any status; In progress rows visible in list.
Professional requisition detail (source 005) shows a 'Close' action via menu. Frontline requisition detail does not expose an explicit close action; Completed status serves this purpose implicitly.

capabilities.name: Create requisition
capabilities.note: NewRequisitionPage component confirmed in source; omnibar create button present in list page.

capabilities.name: Create from template
capabilities.note: TemplatePicker component wired into NewRequisitionPage; 'Apply template' and 'Save as template' actions confirmed in source.

capabilities.name: Bulk import via CSV
capabilities.note: ImportRequisitionPanel component fully implemented with CSV/XLSX file parse, preview step, and apply action.

capabilities.name: View requisition list
capabilities.note: Full paginated list with status tabs (All/Booked/In progress/Completed), ID, status, dates, job assignments, site, suppliers, and est. bill columns.

capabilities.name: View requisition detail
capabilities.note: Detail page with Details, Work assignments, Distribution, Managers, Invoices, and Logs accordion sections confirmed across five requisition screens.

capabilities.name: Search and filter requisitions
capabilities.note: Status tab filter, Date, Job Assignments, and Fulfillment filter chips present. Fulfillment bucket filter (0-25%, 25-75%, etc.) confirmed in source. Feature-flagged columns for billing basis, time capture, supplier types, engagement type also filterable.

capabilities.name: Edit requisition
capabilities.note: Edit button present in omnibar of all detail screens; requisitionEditSchema drives an edit entity panel; toast confirms save.

capabilities.name: Reassign requisition owner
capabilities.note: Reassign bulk action confirmed in source: 'Reassigned N requisitions to a new owner'.

capabilities.name: Duplicate requisition
capabilities.note: Duplicate action in both list row context menu and detail page overflow menu; bulk duplicate also available.

capabilities.name: Delete requisition
capabilities.note: VMS platforms commonly allow deletion of draft/booked requisitions not yet in progress to correct data entry errors. No delete action was found; Cancel is the only destructive option offered once submitted.

capabilities.name: Archive requisition
capabilities.note: VMS commonly archives completed/cancelled requisitions for audit history and spend reporting without cluttering active views. No archive action or archived-status filter is present in the prototype.

capabilities.name: Submit requisition for approval
capabilities.note: Approval workflow engine (source 054) and inbox approval queue (source 042) are fully built. Help-center text states approvals are required before supplier broadcast. However, the new-requisition form does not surface an explicit Submit/Send for approval button; the transition step is backend-implied rather than user-visible in the rendered prototype.

capabilities.name: Approve or reject requisition
capabilities.note: Inbox approval queue shows Approve/Reject action buttons per requisition item with 24-hour SLA counter, escalation, and bulk approve.

capabilities.name: Cancel requisition
capabilities.note: Cancel action with confirmation dialog (listing affected dates and supplier notification) present in list row context menu, detail overflow menu, and bulk actions bar.

capabilities.name: Distribute / broadcast to suppliers
capabilities.note: Activity log records 'broadcast to N suppliers'; Distribution accordion on detail page shows ranked supplier list; bulk 'Distribute' action re-runs distribution for selected requisitions.

capabilities.name: Manage supplier distribution rules per requisition
capabilities.note: Distribution accordion visible on detail page; ReqDistributionBody renders ranked supplier list with note about org-wide setup override.

capabilities.name: View requisition activity log
capabilities.note: Logs accordion on all detail screens; source 040 synthesizes a rich activity timeline (broadcast, candidate submission, worker approval, close-out events).

capabilities.name: Export requisition
capabilities.note: Export summary (.pdf) in detail overflow menu; Export to CSV in bulk actions bar.

capabilities.name: Reschedule requisition dates
capabilities.note: Reschedule appears in bulk overflow menu ('Opening reschedule for N requisitions') but is only a toast placeholder; no reschedule UI exists for single requisitions.

capabilities.name: View fulfillment progress
capabilities.note: Fulfillment filter chip with percent-buckets (0-25%, 25-75%, 75-100%, 100%) in list; fulfillmentPctOf() derives progress from status; Work assignments accordion on detail page.

entities.name: Work Assignment
entities.role: The record that binds a specific worker to a specific shift, tracking the engagement from acceptance through clock-out.

capabilities.name: View work assignment list
capabilities.note: Assignments appear as rows in the Timesheets list (e.g. 'Work assignment #741') with status, worker, supplier, and date filters. However there is no dedicated assignment list view independent of timesheets — browsing is only via the timesheet or requisition work-assignments tab.

capabilities.name: Create work assignment (assign worker to shift)
capabilities.note: Agency admins can assign bench workers to open positions on a requisition via the assign-worker flow. The requisition Work assignments tab is the entry point.

capabilities.name: Edit work assignment details
capabilities.note: Edit is accessible from the chip context menu; edits flow through the requisition form.

capabilities.name: Cancel work assignment
capabilities.note: Cancel work assignment action is visible in the chip context menu with a confirmation dialog.

capabilities.name: Remove worker (unassign) from work assignment
capabilities.note: Remove worker action is surfaced in the shift and worker booking detail headers; reopens the slot to the supplier tier.

capabilities.name: Clock in worker (manual override)
capabilities.note: Manual clock-in is available from the per-row schedule action menu for pre-start assigned rows.

capabilities.name: Edit clock-in time
capabilities.note: Edit clock-in option visible in the schedule row action menu for active assignments.

capabilities.name: Clock out worker (force end shift)
capabilities.note: End shift / clock-out action is available on floor-status rows; locks the timesheet for review.

capabilities.name: Mark worker no-show
capabilities.note: Mark no-show action is available on soon/enroute/flagged rows; flags the shift, notifies supplier and site, and reopens the slot.

capabilities.name: Add time / back-date time entry
capabilities.note: Add time action is available for both pre- and post-clock-in rows from the schedule row action menu.

capabilities.name: Report worker (safety/conduct flag)
capabilities.note: Report worker action sends a flag to supplier and program admin; does not remove the worker.

capabilities.name: Remove worker with reason code and backfill option
capabilities.note: Full remove-worker flow with reason picker (no_show, for_cause, supplier-withdrawal, replaced, etc.), backfill choice (redistribute / replace / leave open / reduce headcount), and optional do-not-rehire flag for eligible reason codes.

capabilities.name: Invite worker via agency (automatic worker invitation)
capabilities.note: Automatic worker invitations are referenced as a feature gated behind the Agency Pro plan. The invited to confirmed lifecycle states are not directly evidenced in rendered walk screens; only the seeded fill model (agency admin manually assigns workers) is visible.

capabilities.name: Extend assignment duration
capabilities.note: An audit log entry in the source references 'extended [worker]'s assignment by 2 weeks' but no dedicated extend UI control is evidenced in the walk renders.

capabilities.name: Delete work assignment record
capabilities.note: VMS systems commonly allow deletion of unfilled or mistakenly created work assignments before any time is recorded, to keep the requisition fill count accurate.

capabilities.name: Archive / close completed work assignments in bulk
capabilities.note: VMS platforms routinely provide bulk archive or batch-close for past assignments to keep active views clean and support audit reporting; no evidence found for this capability.

capabilities.name: View work assignment audit / timeline log
capabilities.note: The timesheet detail (linked to the work assignment) shows a timeline with start, break, and end events including scheduled vs. actual timestamps.

entities.name: Worker
entities.role: A non-employee individual (agency labor, contractor, consultant, or EOR worker) whose identity, eligibility, skills, and engagement history are tracked in the system.

transitions.action: credential expires or enters expiry window

capabilities.name: List and search workers
capabilities.note: Workforce list with search field, pool tabs (All / Agency), and filter chips for Credential status, Supplier, and Job. Status counts (Available now, On assignment, Credential watch) are surfaced as quick-filter badges.

capabilities.name: View worker detail
capabilities.note: Worker identity and engagement data is readable from the workforce list and referenced in timesheet and invoice detail screens. Source exposes a WorkerDetailsPage with professional and frontline accordion sections.

capabilities.name: Invite / add worker
capabilities.note: Source contains an 'Invite Frontline worker' flow (multi-step: invite method, email/phone, onboarding tasks, review) and an 'Add Professional' panel. The workforce help page confirms both paths. The workforce omnibar surfaces 'Invite Frontline worker'.

capabilities.name: Edit worker profile
capabilities.note: Source references rate-edit controls on the professional detail panel and custom-field editing on the worker object, but no dedicated 'Edit worker' screen was observed in the walk renders. Float worker profiles are explicitly read-only in Flex Work (edits round-trip through Dayforce core).

capabilities.name: Onboard worker
capabilities.note: Full onboarding flow evidenced in source: step-based panel (invite code, RTW/I-9, W-4/P45, banking, orientation), onboarding task catalog per job, required-item gate for shift readiness. Onboarding status badge appears on the workforce list.

capabilities.name: Offboard worker
capabilities.note: Source exposes getOffboardingTasksForWorker(), an offboarding checklist (asset return, badge deactivation, mobile access removal, DNR flag), and an 'Offboard / deactivate' help-center entry. Worker status transitions to 'Offboarding' and then 'Inactive' or 'Terminated'.

capabilities.name: Assign worker to shift / requisition
capabilities.note: Schedule view shows named workers (Jamal Carter, Makenna Herwitz) assigned to shifts. Source exposes openAssignWorkerPanel() and a drag-to-reassign flow. Status transitions to 'On Assignment'.

capabilities.name: Message worker
capabilities.note: Source exposes a 'Message <worker first name>' quick action on the schedule row menu (showToast confirms the thread opens). No rendered messaging screen was captured in the walk files.

capabilities.name: Flag worker as Do-Not-Return (DNR)
capabilities.note: Source contains a DNR offboarding task item ('Policy-gated. Only specific reason codes may set it.') and a config option 'dnrRequiresHr'. No dedicated DNR UI screen was observed in the walk renders.

capabilities.name: View worker credential / compliance status
capabilities.note: Workforce list exposes a 'Credential status' filter chip with values Compliant / Onboarding / Expired. Source shows a credential watchlist panel, expiry-alert inbox items, and a hard-block on bookings past credential expiry. Help text confirms the rollup reads from Dayforce Credentialing.

capabilities.name: View worker engagement history (shifts, timesheets)
capabilities.note: Workers appear on the Timesheets list and on Invoice line items. The timesheet detail (022) shows a worker's clock-in/out timeline for a named assignment. Source exposes shifts, assignments, and hours on the worker object.

capabilities.name: Delete / permanently remove worker record
capabilities.note: VMS systems retain worker records for audit trail, co-employment compliance, and tenure-cap enforcement — hard deletes are rarely permitted. No delete action was found in the walk or source. Expected as an admin-only capability to satisfy data-subject erasure requests (GDPR Art. 17).

capabilities.name: Archive / deactivate worker record
capabilities.note: Source exposes a 'Deactivate' action on the worker row menu (transitions to 'Inactive') and a 'Reactivate' action. The offboard/deactivate help-center entry confirms both paths. Worker status enum includes 'Inactive' and 'Terminated'.

capabilities.name: Transition worker through lifecycle states
capabilities.note: Onboarding to Compliant to Active to On Assignment to Offboarding/Inactive/Terminated are all evidenced in source. The On Leave sub-state exists in the status enum but no explicit 'mark on leave / return' UI was observed. The Credential Watch / Expired state is surfaced as a list filter and inbox alert but no explicit re-activation-after-renewal transition screen was found.

capabilities.name: Rate / score worker after engagement
capabilities.note: Redeployment rate is called out in the help center as the highest-leverage program metric, implying worker performance signals are needed. A VMS commonly allows managers to leave a post-assignment rating on a worker to inform future booking decisions and talent-pool prioritisation. No rating or scoring UI for workers was observed in the walk or source.

capabilities.name: Convert worker to permanent employee
capabilities.note: Source mentions 'Conversion to permanent' as a named workflow triggered when a tenure cap is hit, and a contractor SOW 'terminate engagement' action carries a 'convert to permanent' branch. No full conversion-to-perm screen was captured in the walk renders; the roadmap lists 'Alumni / boomerang ATS bridge' as NEXT, suggesting the full flow is incomplete.
